name: Alliance Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank headquartered in St. Paul, Minnesota, offering checking, savings, mortgages, business banking, and agricultural lending across six branch locations.
description_long:
Alliance Bank is a regional FDIC-insured bank based in St. Paul, Minnesota, with a primary focus on serving individual consumers, small businesses, and agricultural clients. The bank operates six physical branch locations across Minnesota (St. Paul, Madelia, Hanska, New Ulm, Lake City, and Red Wing) and emphasizes community banking with dedicated personal service. Founded to serve the unique needs of rural and agricultural communities, Alliance Bank has developed specialized expertise in farm lending alongside traditional retail banking products.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including checking and savings accounts, credit cards, online banking, mobile banking with Zelle money transfer integration, mortgage loans (with home buying resources), business payroll solutions via SurePayroll, cash management tools, and financial calculators. Their Individual Banking division provides full-service consumer products, while their Business Banking and Agricultural Lending divisions cater to small business owners and farmers with specialized expertise and personalized attention.

Alliance Bank distinguishes itself through deep agricultural lending expertise, claiming "Nobody knows agricultural banking better," and through emphasis on personal service across small branch locations. The bank actively promotes security features (mobile banking safety tips), consumer education (home buying checklists), and modern digital tools (24/7 online banking, Zelle integration). Their positioning targets relationship banking rather than pure convenience or low-cost commodity banking.

The main limitation is geographic scope: operations are confined to Minnesota with only six physical branch locations, limiting accessibility for customers outside this region. As a regional bank, they may have fewer digital-only conveniences compared to national neobanks or large national banks, though specific product features, fee structures, and competitive rates are not disclosed on the public website. The bank does not serve California customers for account opening (CCPA disclosure references suggest previous California service under a different name).
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government guarantee
Specialized agricultural lending expertise and dedicated farm business support
SurePayroll integration offering 6 months free for small business payroll
Zelle integration for fast, secure peer-to-peer money transfers via mobile app
24/7 online banking access from anywhere with full account management
Free financial calculators for savings rate and loan payment estimation
Six physical Minnesota branch locations with dedicated personal service
Comprehensive individual, business, and agricultural product lines in one institution
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Minnesota only—inaccessible for out-of-state customers
Only six physical branch locations limits convenience compared to regional or national banks
Website does not disclose specific interest rates, APYs, fees, or product pricing for comparison
No mention of investment products, wealth management, or advanced financial services
Limited information about mobile app features beyond Zelle and basic security

contact.address: 105 E Lyon Ave, Lake City, MN 55041

best_for: Minnesota-based farmers and agricultural business owners needing specialized lending
Small business owners in Minnesota seeking integrated payroll and cash management tools
Minnesota consumers wanting relationship banking with personal service and community focus
Individuals seeking FDIC-insured checking/savings with mobile banking convenience
diagnosis: Alliance Bank is best for Minnesota-based small business owners, farmers, or individual consumers seeking relationship-oriented community banking with FDIC insurance, modern digital tools, and specialized agricultural expertise. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to Minnesota with only six branches, making it unsuitable for customers outside this region or those prioritizing maximum branch/ATM convenience.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 105 E Lyon Ave, Lake City, MN 55041
